briefdescription.para: Carry-propagate across 16 limbs of a GF(2^255-19) element.
Constant-time conditional swap of p and q (if b=1).
Reduce and serialise a GF element to 32 bytes (little-endian).
Deserialise 32 bytes into a GF element.

memberdef.argsstring: (gf o, const gf a, const gf b)
memberdef.name: A

memberdef.argsstring: (gf o, const gf a, const gf b)
memberdef.name: Z

memberdef.argsstring: (gf o, const gf a, const gf b)
memberdef.name: M

briefdescription.para: Field multiplication: o = a * b mod p.
Field squaring: o = a^2 mod p.
Field inversion: o = a^(-1) mod p via Fermat's little theorem.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint8_t shared_out[32], const uint8_t scalar[32], const uint8_t point[32])
memberdef.name: syn_x25519

detaileddescription.para: Computes shared = scalar * point on Curve25519.

briefdescription.para: Derive a public key from a private key.

detaileddescription.para: Computes public = scalar * basepoint (the Curve25519 generator, u=9).
Based on the TweetNaCl approach: 16 × int64_t limbs (16 bits each). Simpler and more auditable than 10-limb donna at the cost of some speed. The entire scalar multiplication takes ~10M multiplies.
On Cortex-M4 @ 64 MHz this is ~2-4 seconds — acceptable since handshakes only happen every ~2 minutes.
